title: One-binary S3: deployed-mode no-rebuild guarantee — ACS predicate forbidding new runtime executable-build sites + target-repo integration test
summary: OPERATOR-BOOSTED to second-block priority 2026-07-13. APPROVED PLAN slice 3/5 (dep S1). After S1, the ONLY remaining executable-build site must be go/internal/releasepipeline/releasepipeline.go:601 (operator-invoked self-release; never fires in target-repo cycles). Pin the invariant: (1) durable ACS predicate scanning go/internal + go/cmd (excluding releasepipeline and _test.go) for runtime executable-build patterns ('go build -o', sysexec 'go','build' arg sequences) — RED if any new site appears; (2) integration test (//go:build integration): run a cycle against a temp target repo WITHOUT .apicover-enforce and assert no first-party executable is created or replaced anywhere in the temp tree AND the running os.Executable() hash/mtime is unchanged after the cycle. This is the kernel-level guarantee corporate security can rely on: the approved fingerprint cannot drift during operation.
acceptance: ACS predicate RED-tests by injecting a dummy 'go build -o' call site in a scratch package
go test -race PASS; go test -tags integration ./cmd/evolve/ PASS; apicover clean
